features.name: Local LLM Chat
features.description: Text-generation request/session/service types, native-tool availability/default policy, native tool contract/toolbox, calculator, datetime, web search, URL fetch, weather tools, tool-calling coordinator, and gpt-oss harmony stream parsing are packaged; MLX runtime adapters, model download/selection UI, chat UI, and tool-run UI/persistence mapping remain in the app. Camera (iOS) used to capture images for multimodal attach; photo attach uses PhotosPicker (no TCC).

goldStandard.gap: MLX runtime adapters, model download/selection UI, chat UI + tool-run UI/persistence still app-side; app imports packaged types directly, no backend registry seam yet

features.name: Transcription (Whisper / SpeechAnalyzer)
features.description: Core request/result models, request source/mode/translation flag, scheduler, scheduling priority, runtime lane resolver/OpenAI-model exclusion policy, Apple engine selection/fallback policy, concurrency profile/configuration/status, device capability detection helper, job service, Whisper language picker/display/request-parameter presentation, Settings speech-language display/code groups, Settings transcription model-card presentation, Transcribe playback/recording duration formatting, and the Whisper model catalog (sizes, WhisperKit variant/tokenizer manifests, device-availability policy, stored-selection normalization, preference-driven selection ordering) are packaged in LocalAITranscription; the app runtime imports the packaged types directly while supplying app-owned feature-flag/availability inputs. TERMINAL: only the WhisperKit/Apple SpeechAnalyzer/SFSpeechRecognizer live adapters, recording UI, model-selection UI layout, localized model presentation (display names/descriptions), the filesystem-bound WhisperModelAssets, history, and global-transcription shell remain — irreducible app-shell.

goldStandard.gap: product logic + catalog packaged, but app imports packaged types directly and the WhisperKit/Apple live engine adapters + recording/model-selection UI are app-shell-terminal — no registry seam

features.name: Wav2Vec2 XLSR-53 Multilingual Transcription
features.description: PARKED to purgatory 2026-07-08 — likely deletion candidate. Ships nowhere and FEATURE_WAV2VEC2 remains defined on no shipping target. The macOS-only FeatureWav2Vec2 package makes the architecture seam complete, but does not change the distribution wall: scripts/build-wav2vec2-runtime.sh still compiles the py-ai-dev/wav2vec2.cpp CLI plus a Python conversion environment, no pre-converted GGUFs are published, and a self-compiled unsigned subprocess cannot ship in notarized/App-Store builds. File-transcription-only, ~3× RTF. Revive only with an in-process static C target (no subprocess/signing) or an otherwise sanctioned compile/sign/distribution path.

caveats.note: Not shipped — App Store (sandboxed) build excludes the subprocess runtime.
File-transcription lane only. Requires the optional wav2vec2-cli runtime installed via scripts/build-wav2vec2-runtime.sh; engine reports unavailable-with-reason until then.

File-transcription lane only (RTF ~3×, slower than real time — never wired to live mic). 16-language xlsr-53 family, per-language GGUF converted via python3. macOS only (Process/subprocess); iOS excluded.

goldStandard.gap: LocalAITranscription owns pure selection/catalog/parser/runtime contracts; macOS-only FeatureWav2Vec2 owns AVFoundation resampling, subprocess lifecycle, availability, and optional-engine registration. The app shell consumes only the feature-agnostic transcription-engine registry.

features.name: Global Transcription (menu bar)
features.description: macOS only. FeatureGlobalTranscription owns GlobalTranscriptionService session state and public lifecycle, queue/orchestration policy, GlobalTranscriptionFeatureController/LaunchParticipant, the live .menuBar contribution and detailed engine/background controls, TranscriptionWindowManager plus loading/recording/status views, and FeatureAutoPaste completion orchestration. The package participant, contribution factory, service, and window manager are the sole live owners; the former app service/window/menu paths are removed. The app injects Whisper/audio/history runtime effects, Carbon/NSEvent hotkey registration, CGWindow active-window capture, TCC permission checks/requests, one NSPanel host, and clipboard/CGEvent/notification paste effects. APPLE PERMISSION DIFFERENCE: Outdoor uses an NSEvent global monitor for a configurable modifier-only hotkey (default Right ⌘), gated on Accessibility (AXIsProcessTrusted); Indoor uses Carbon RegisterEventHotKey for a fixed ⌃⌥Space with no special permission. Screen Recording remains required for active-window title routing. PENDING: hotkey, menu-bar, panel, permission, active-window capture, and paste verification in a real macOS session.

caveats.note: Fixed hotkey ⌃⌥Space via Carbon RegisterEventHotKey — NOT configurable, no Accessibility. Screen Recording still requested for active-window title routing.
Configurable modifier-only hotkey (default Right ⌘) via NSEvent global monitor, gated on Accessibility (AXIsProcessTrusted).

goldStandard.gap: service/session state, panel controller/views, detailed menu controls, launch lifecycle, and paste orchestration are packaged; app adapters terminate at Whisper/audio/history, NSPanel, clipboard/CGEvent, notifications, and TCC/window capture

features.name: Auto-paste transcription into active app
features.description: macOS. INDOOR DIFFERENCE: Indoor clipboard-only; Outdoor auto-pastes via Accessibility. FeatureAutoPaste owns completion policy, manual-paste preview, destination-capture delay, and copy/paste/prompt/wait/capture orchestration. The app shell only supplies platform side-effect adapters for clipboard writes, CGEvent paste simulation, notifications, HUD, and destination capture.

caveats.note: Not shipped (sandbox + no Accessibility); transcription paste falls back to clipboard-only.
Auto-pastes into the active app via Accessibility + CGEvent paste simulation.

goldStandard.gap: all orchestration packaged with only thin platform side-effect adapters left, but wired directly rather than registered through a provider/registry like the reference module

features.name: Window management of other apps
features.description: macOS. Unsandboxed only. Hotkey mapping, frame geometry, best-effort frame clamping, other-app snap/action frame planning, drag-release pointer/frame edge/corner detection across physical and visible screen frames, own-window planning, multi-screen detection policy, manual-accessibility app policy, settings availability/shortcut/subtitle presentation, AXWindowMover, and ScreenSnapshotting contracts are packaged. WindowManagementHotkeyController and the settings view consume the injected mover; screen detection consumes the injected snapshot provider. Accessibility lookup/movement, NSScreen capture, NSEvent monitoring, and the System Settings deep-link remain thin app adapters so App Store builds keep Accessibility API references out of the target path. The existing `.windowManagement` UIContribution ID, slot, order, and gate remain unchanged.

caveats.note: Not shipped — App Store build keeps Accessibility API references out of the target path.
Snap/move OTHER apps' windows via Accessibility (AXIsProcessTrusted). Unsandboxed only.

goldStandard.gap: planning and provider contracts are packaged; AX movement and NSScreen capture remain injected terminal app adapters behind the shared settings contribution

features.name: Window management (own windows)
features.description: macOS. FeatureWindowManagement owns hotkey mapping, frame geometry, own-window eligibility, target-window selection priority, resize planning, AppKit/AX coordinate conversion, same-relative-position display cycling, and settings shortcut/subtitle presentation. The app shell only snapshots NSWindow/NSScreen state, applies the planned frame, and composes the settings UI.

goldStandard.gap: all planning logic packaged with only NSWindow/NSScreen snapshot + frame-apply adapters left, but composed directly rather than via a registry/provider seam

features.name: Quit Apps (force-quit running apps)
features.description: macOS, Outdoor only (unsandboxed). FeatureQuitApps owns running-app classification, exclusion policy, app snapshotting, terminate/escalate policy, view model, window, and SwiftUI surface. The app shell only gates availability through FeatureFlags/Pro access and calls the package presenter.

caveats.note: Not shipped — sandbox blocks terminating other processes.
Force-quit running apps; Outdoor-only and Pro-gated. No special TCC.

goldStandard.gap: entire surface (logic + view model + SwiftUI window) packaged; app shell is gate-and-call only, but invoked directly rather than registered through a provider/registry

features.name: Screen Awake
features.description: macOS menu bar. FeatureScreenAwake owns duration policy, session state, Pomodoro state, observable controller, IOKit power/clamshell orchestration, notification/event seams, menu-bar UI, status observer, and termination cleanup primitive. The app shell only supplies diagnostics logging/lid-state metadata and startup registration.

goldStandard.gap: full controller + IOKit orchestration + menu-bar UI packaged; only diagnostics/startup adapters remain, but registered via direct startup wiring not a provider seam

features.name: Keyboard Backlight
features.description: macOS menu bar, Outdoor/Beta only. FeatureKeyboardBacklight owns the CoreBrightness private-framework bridge, service state, ON/OFF restore policy, last-brightness persistence key, manual-vs-ambient auto state transitions, and SwiftUI menu row. The package is linked only by Direct/Outdoor and macOS Beta targets, never by App Store targets.

caveats.note: Not shipped — CoreBrightness is a private framework, so the package is not linked by App Store targets.
Controls the built-in MacBook keyboard backlight through a runtime-loaded CoreBrightness bridge.

goldStandard.gap: service, pure control logic, persistence keys, and menu-bar UI are packaged; app shell only links/registers the feature on Outdoor/Beta

features.name: Hide Desktop Icons / Desktop Curtain
features.description: macOS menu bar. Sandbox-safe overlay NSWindow at desktopIcon+1 (above icons, below app windows) covering the desktop icons (frost / wallpaper / Catty 3D / solid). The Catty 3D ambient scene is the cat-themed fallback shown when a custom wallpaper can't be read in the sandbox. Package FeatureDesktopCurtain owns the fill policy, generic cat-content hook, fallback image, and overlay controller; the app shell owns the Catty content provider, menu toggle, and DesktopCurtainService persistence.

caveats.note: Overlay covers the icons (sandbox-safe); it does not genuinely remove them. Genuine CreateDesktop hiding isn't possible in the App Store sandbox and is intentionally not used.

goldStandard.gap: FeatureDesktopCurtain owns the fill policy (unit-tested) + generic app-provided cat-themed content hook (behavioural-tested) + AppKit overlay controller incl. the covering window-level (behavioural-tested); app shell supplies the Catty 3D ambient view, menu-bar toggle, and persisted DesktopCurtainService.

features.name: Eye Care reminder (20-20-20)
features.description: macOS menu bar. FeatureEyeCare owns metrics storage, reminder controller/state machine, interval/look-away/Pomodoro/goodbye timer behavior, Cat Nap controller/state machine, lid/sleep reconciliation, Skip challenge handoff policy, screen-time tracking policy, settings content, settings metrics visibility, presentation copy, panel content, panel controllers, and Dangerously Skip menu/settings presentation content. The app shell supplies lifecycle observers, keyboard-locking side effects, Doug/panel notifications, optional asset preloading, Skip service injection/Bluetooth/session side effects, clipboard adapter, settings sheets/remaining feature gates, and startup registration.

caveats.note: Break-time keyboard-cleaning uses a frontmost NSPanel overlay (no Accessibility); degraded — keys that bypass a frontmost panel aren't blocked.
Break-time keyboard-cleaning intercepts all keys system-wide via a CGEvent .defaultTap gated on Accessibility.

goldStandard.gap: controllers/state machines/presentation packaged, but app shell still wires many side effects (keyboard-lock, notifications, Skip service injection, settings sheets/gates) directly rather than via a registry

features.name: Monitor Control (external DDC)
features.description: macOS. FeatureMonitorControl owns DDC display contracts/backend, display controller state/read/write orchestration, CoreAudio output, write coalescing, HUD/menu/settings SwiftUI primitives, startup participants, display-selection/status/settings presentation, selected-display persistence helper, focused-display geometry resolution, media-key activation policy, event tap/parser/action routing, display volume policy, media-key permission presentation/lifecycle policy, and the display-environment/Accessibility-request provider contracts. Settings and menu-bar placement use the shared UIContribution seam with their existing IDs. The app keeps feature/defaults value reads, build-flavor injection, the concrete settings key, notification observers, and thin AppKit screen/mouse plus PermissionManager adapters. DDC/CI brightness over I2C needs no permission; media-key routing (Outdoor only) uses a CGEvent .defaultTap gated on Accessibility (AXIsProcessTrusted).

caveats.note: DDC/CI brightness over I2C works (no permission). Media-key volume/brightness routing UNAVAILABLE — needs a CGEvent .defaultTap + Accessibility.
Full: DDC brightness + media-key volume/brightness routing via CGEvent .defaultTap gated on Accessibility.

goldStandard.gap: DDC/media-key behavior and UI are packaged; AppKit display snapshots and Accessibility prompting are injected terminal adapters behind package protocols

features.name: Compute / Model Link (discovery, remote cat, P2P, VNC + audio, SSH, Terminal3D)
features.description: Cross-platform. FeatureCompute owns per-device host-profile, saved-computer, recent-connection, Catty favorite-folder, manual-host, Remote Cat credential, SSH credential, and VNC credential stores, SSH handoff command policy, the versioned PCM/multipart/jitter/security contract for the app-peer VNC audio side-channel, pure Tailscale status parsing/mapping, Tailscale CLI path/argument/environment policy, Tailscale section subtitle/state presentation, Tailscale serve DNS/funnel status policy, and tailnet service probe response classification/model-list decoding. Shared discovery contracts, Compute persistence models, and P2P Model Link peer/Bonjour/loopback contract are packaged in LocalAIContracts; VNC UI is packaged in RoyalVNCKitUI. Networking discovery/probing/cloud sync, Tailscale CLI process execution, tailnet HTTPS transport/TLS fingerprint capture, P2P advertise/browse/relay transport, sharing orchestration, audio capture/playback and VNC-session UI, SSH Terminal/pasteboard side effects, Terminal3D shell-spawn, and Compute UI remain app-owned. Two-way audio is a bearer-token-gated `/v1` side-channel, appears only for `hasModelServer` peers, prefers Tailscale HTTPS/WireGuard, and tears down with VNC; plain RFB remains screen-only. Terminal3D shell-spawn is Outdoor macOS only (→ iOS partial); remote-cat and iOS AWDL P2P model surfaces are contributed through the ChatBackend registry (ModelCatalogContributor), so the runtime catalog no longer injects them inline. TERMINAL: the remaining app-owned networking/transport/SSH/Terminal3D/VNC and Compute UI are irreducible app-shell. Apple Events (NSAppleScript) opens Terminal.app for SSH on Outdoor macOS.

caveats.note: Partial — saved + iCloud-synced hosts and VNC/SSH-to-saved-host only. Outdoor-only and stripped here: Tailscale CLI live status/serve/funnel, Terminal3D shell-spawn, and SSH-into-Terminal.app (NSAppleScript). Local Network (Bonjour) permission.

Full: Tailscale CLI, P2P AWDL Model Link, VNC with app-peer two-way audio, SSH via Terminal.app (Apple Events), Terminal3D. Audio uses Local Network + microphone + Screen Recording/system-audio permission.

goldStandard.gap: model surfaces contributed via the ChatBackend/ModelCatalogContributor registry, but networking/transport/SSH/Terminal3D/VNC + Compute UI remain a large app-owned (irreducible) shell

features.name: OpenClaw (gateway chat)
features.description: REVIVED on Beta 2026-06-18 — out of purgatory. FeatureOpenClaw (gateway backend, probe, credential store, model contributor, workspace state, chat workspace view, pairing/pinned/history/home/compute UI primitives) + OpenClawKit are linked on the macOS Beta target ONLY; FEATURE_OPENCLAW (defined on the Beta target and in FeatureOpenClaw's Package.swift) activates the fenced gateway-chat surfaces. Physically absent from Outdoor + App Store binaries, same mechanism as BetaTelemetry. modular=yes: product logic and OpenClaw UI ownership now live in FeatureOpenClaw; the app shell retains only thin build-fence/navigation/storage adapters where the package is not linked in production targets. iOS pairing paths exist but it only ever activated on macOS (→ iOS partial).

caveats.note: Not shipped — physically absent from the binary (package not linked).
Not shipped — physically absent from the binary (package not linked).

Beta-only: gateway chat via FEATURE_OPENCLAW on the macOS Beta target. Local Network permission. Needs a reachable OpenClaw v4 gateway + token. iOS pairing paths exist but never activate (→ iOS partial).

features.name: Local API server
features.description: macOS. HTTP routing, public docs/OpenAPI/health/Ollama-liveness endpoint response policy, runtime route-forwarding policy, request/response transport parsing/serialization, multipart parsing, upload content-type extension policy, model list/status responses, transcription request parameter mapping, recording-session route classification, verbose transcription response model, settings defaults, port fallback, build-injected token requirement default, token persistence/redaction/generation/reset policy, network-exposure flag, idle-unload policy/label, menu-bar status/accessibility presentation, Settings server status-row presentation, start-failure/port-conflict presentation, request-log filtering/path sanitization/auth-state classification, agent-skill Markdown/auth/include-token generation/copy hint, OpenAPI JSON generation, status/docs page HTML, LocalAPIListenerControlling, and LocalAPIListenerLaunchParticipant are packaged. INDOOR DIFFERENCE: token required by default in Indoor. The app retains NWListener binding, notification delivery, settings composition, menu-bar side effects, and microphone recording execution; startup and termination now resolve that adapter lazily through packaged launch participants. The existing `.localAPI` UIContribution ID, Local API & Automation group, order, availability, and visible settings surface remain unchanged. Localhost only unless network exposure is explicitly enabled.

caveats.note: API token REQUIRED by default (defaultRequiresToken: true). Localhost only unless network exposure is explicitly enabled.
API token NOT required by default. Localhost only unless exposed.

goldStandard.gap: HTTP behavior and listener lifecycle contract are packaged; NWListener binding remains an injected terminal app adapter behind shared launch and settings contribution seams

features.name: App Control API
features.description: macOS alpha lane. Bearer-authenticated /v1/local/app routes control the real visible app. AppControlKit owns the wire descriptors, registry actor, SwiftUI page-root modifier, injected-clock transaction state machine, and NavigationDestinationProviding, AXWalking, and AppCapturing seams. The app shell injects its typed destination catalog/navigation adapter, in-process accessibility walker, concrete capture composition, and HTTP route provider. The Local API provider list remains the sole route-registration owner. Settings root and Translate hub are the initial declarative registry surfaces; feature/API coverage remains partial while all other catalogued surfaces honestly report AX or unavailable coverage.

caveats.note: Read-only discovery/state plus alpha-gated navigation and actions. Declarative coverage starts with Settings and Translate; remaining surfaces use self-AX fallback.

goldStandard.gap: AppControlKit owns the navigation, AX-walking, and capture contracts plus descriptors, registry, and transactions; platform composition is injected through app conformers

features.name: Routines
features.description: Alpha P5. RoutineKit owns revisioned routines, session lifecycle, injected-clock scheduling, DST and missed-session recovery, atomic persistence, idempotent commands/provider updates, screen-time thresholds, reconnectable events, and the pure planned-day planner (DayPlanParameters solved into typed blocks around real meetings: meals, indoor/outdoor exercise, deep work, leisure, overnight-agent slot, wind-down, sleep, ambient eye-care ticks). The app composes existing macOS providers, projects EventKit and iOS 26 AlarmKit records as recoverable subordinate state, exposes linked iOS views through UUID-deduplicated commands based on monotonic App Group snapshot revisions, and serves the day plan over token-authenticated /v1/local/day-plan routes (parameters, meetings import, block decline/move/swap, re-plan) that mutate the same observable store the calendar UI renders — API edits re-render the open calendar live.

caveats.note: P4 links revisioned snapshots and commands through the existing App Group and projects eligible iOS 26 sessions into AlarmKit. Alarm actions open the session; exercise verification remains P5.
P5 planned-day planner: the day arrives already made (decline, don't accept). Eye care renders as ambient ticks, not blocks; runtime EyeCareService nudge gating by meeting/away state is follow-up.

goldStandard.gap: engine mutations, providers, recoverable calendar/alarm projections, screen-time thresholds, the pure planned-day planner, and the revisioned App Group command bridge are isolated behind tested package contracts

features.name: MLXServe sidecar
features.description: macOS Outdoor/Beta only. FeatureMLXServeSidecar owns configuration, settings reads, model/cache path resolution, subprocess launch/supervision, health polling, model discovery, log rotation, status state, and lazy startup/termination participants. The app injects only main-Bundle resolution for Contents/Resources/mlxserve/mlxserve-http and AppLog forwarding. The new focused package was chosen instead of folding the operationally independent subprocess into the embedded MLXServe module. FEATURE_MLXSERVE_SIDECAR still bundles mlxserve-http plus mlx.metallib only when MLXSERVE_BINARY_DIR is supplied; missing artifacts still report binary not bundled. The sidecar remains the same default-off second listener on port 11435, points at the same LLM and WhisperKit roots, and leaves legacy :11434 untouched. Its settings remain in exactly the same visible position inside the existing `.localAPI` UIContribution; no separate sidecar row or gate was introduced. The A/B phase deliberately permits double-loading because the sidecar process and app runtime pool are separate until serving unification.

caveats.note: Not shipped — App Store and iOS targets do not resolve the local-path mlxserve dependency.
Experimental second listener on port 11435. Default off; runs the real bundled mlxserve-http engine out-of-process.

goldStandard.gap: controller, process supervision, health/model discovery, settings policy, and lifecycle participant are packaged; the app injects only Bundle resolution and log forwarding

features.name: MLXServe embedded listener
features.description: Cross-platform default-off A/B infrastructure. Adds FEATURE_MLXSERVE_EMBED and links only the MLXServeHTTP product from local-ai-cat/mlxserve. The adapter implements mlxserve's OpenAIChatBackend over the app's existing GenerationQueue/TextGenerationRuntime lineage, preserving the single resident model runtime invariant. Start and termination are registered through LaunchParticipant and receive the app runtime's shared GenerationQueue handle through injection. macOS keeps the out-of-process sidecar as the default A/B path; iOS can opt into an in-process loopback listener on port 11435 for device validation.

caveats.note: Compiled as default-off A/B infrastructure; no review-facing surface and no listener unless explicitly enabled.
Compiled as default-off A/B infrastructure; alpha-lane settings can enable the in-process listener on 127.0.0.1:11435.

goldStandard.gap: terminal app backend adapter remains; listener lifecycle is registered through LaunchParticipant with the shared serving handle injected

features.name: Energy & Impact
features.description: Estimator, ledger, fleet aggregation, citations, unit models, display formatting, ledger file persistence, privacy-preserving device identity, iCloud KVS sync, backfill computation, recorder engine, value-typed Energy UI content, methodology view, and mock/offscreen presentation are packaged. The app shell retains startup wiring, memory-history backfill adapter, static recording hooks, Settings navigation, and live recorder wrapper. iPhone + Mac power measurement.

goldStandard.gap: estimator/ledger/UI fully packaged with only thin adapters left, but startup + live recorder wrapper are wired directly rather than registered through a provider/registry

features.name: Dangerously Skip (BLE jump rope)
features.description: New in 1.3.x. Renpho/R-Q008 rope. SkipKit owns codec, challenge state, completion stats, transport seam, CoreBluetooth transport, and service orchestration; FeatureDangerouslySkip owns the live service facade, registry, overlay controller, menu/settings adapters, the macOS Utilities Skip screen, and the iOS standalone session screen; FeatureEyeCare owns shared macOS menu/settings presentation content. The app shell keeps composition-root registration, feature/defaults value injection, panel routing, and settings/menu placement. Surfaced through the Utilities hub on macOS and iOS.

caveats.note: Included in Indoor: the macOS screen block is a permission-free overlay (no Accessibility API), and iOS standalone sessions can only ship through the App Store lane.
Surfaced through the Outdoor macOS Utilities hub.

goldStandard.gap: codec/transport/orchestration in SkipKit, live feature facade/registry plus service adapters in FeatureDangerouslySkip, and presentation primitives in FeatureEyeCare

features.name: Lyrics Player (Apple Music + live translation)
features.description: Play Apple Music (ApplicationMusicPlayer via MusadoraKit on macOS) and read lyrics translated live — whole-line contextual translation plus a per-word interlinear gloss. Lyrics come from the community LRCLIB database. Translation prefers the on-device Foundation Model (Apple Intelligence, no language-pack download) and falls back to Apple's Translation framework; both engines are vendored INTO FeatureLyrics so the feature stays App-Store-decoupled and self-contained. Core also compiles for iOS (companion mode), but ships macOS-only since there is no iOS Outdoor target.

caveats.note: NEVER linked into an App Store build — crowd-sourced lyrics (LRCLIB) are an App Store rights risk. FeatureLyrics is attached only to the Outdoor + Beta macOS targets; the TranslateHub card is behind `#if canImport(FeatureLyrics)` so App Store builds compile it out.
Surfaced as a card on the Translate hub (dev/beta hub). Requires Apple Music access (NSAppleMusicUsageDescription) + an Apple Music subscription for catalog search/playback.

features.package: FeatureLyrics (self-contained: playback + LRCLIB lyrics + Apple/FoundationModels translation) + FeatureTranslateDomain (shared value types)

goldStandard.gap: playback/lyrics/translation/UI all packaged in FeatureLyrics; the app shell only wires the Translate-hub card. The private Apple syllable-lyrics source (MusanovaKit) is a documented follow-up, not yet integrated.

features.name: Workout: Exercise (camera rep counter)
features.description: The workout-gate concept, native: an ExerciseObserver emits phase observations, ExerciseKit's RepCounter/ExerciseChallenge count reps with hysteresis + anti-cheat (minimum rep duration, confidence gates, liveness). Current slice ships both a deterministic demo observer and a live AVCapture/Vision body-pose observer through ExerciseCameraRunner/VisionPoseExerciseObserver, so camera permission is required. Camera→VLM observer (CameraFrameSource + VLMDispatchKit) remains a later enhancement.

caveats.note: Not surfaced in Indoor — the Utilities hub stays hidden in App Store builds.
Surfaced through the Outdoor macOS Utilities hub with live camera and demo observers.

goldStandard.gap: rep counting/anti-cheat/challenge state fully packaged in ExerciseKit; the app owns only the Utilities screen and (later) the camera→VLM observer adapter

features.name: Sentry Mode (away-guard lock)
features.description: Machine-locked away-guard: arm it, walk away; key/mouse activity raises the wallpaper curtain + a key-swallowing lock panel, sustained activity starts a screen recording and a spoken cat taunt, the passphrase disarms. SentryKit is the pure clock-injected brain emitting intents; mouse can't be blocked on macOS by design — the curtain + keyboard-swallowing panel is the effective lock.

caveats.note: Not surfaced in Indoor — the Utilities hub stays hidden in App Store builds; keylog-adjacent surface parked pending a 2.4.5 review read.
Surfaced through the Outdoor macOS Utilities hub. Input Monitoring (listen-only tap) + Screen Recording TCC prompts on first arm.

features.package: SentryKit + app adapters (key monitor, lock panel, curtain, recorder)

goldStandard.gap: the escalation ladder/state machine is fully packaged in SentryKit; the app owns the impure edges (CGEvent listen-only tap, blocking panel, curtain, ScreenCaptureKit recorder, speech quirk)

features.name: Model Link (P2P remote AI)
features.description: Reach a nearby Mac's local AI without Wi-Fi (peer-to-peer / Tailnet). FeatureModelLink owns RemoteCatBackend, P2PModelLinkService, ModelLinkShareService, TailnetServiceProber, ChatBackendRegistry model/generator registration, and the TailscaleCLIRunning plus KVSyncing provider contracts. The app injects TailscaleServeService for CLI process execution and ComputerCloudSync for iCloud KVS publication, plus existing DiagnosticLogger and Local API settings/token adapters. The existing `.modelLinkSharing` UIContribution ID remains in the same Settings group/order with the same availability; its row still opens the same Model Link page, and the Share over Tailscale controls retain their existing position inside Local API settings.

caveats.note: Not surfaced in Indoor — Model Link remains an alpha-lane feature.
Surfaced through Settings beside Local API and local-coding controls.

goldStandard.gap: remote backend, P2P transport, sharing orchestration, tailnet probing, provider contracts, and chat-backend registration are packaged; CLI and KVS side effects remain terminal app adapters

features.name: Quick Capture (capture-chord palette)
features.description: macOS Alpha only. FeatureQuickCapture owns the native palette, target/area pickers, chord arbitration, view model, concrete runtime coordinator and recording ownership, live Utilities UIContribution, and QuickCaptureLaunchParticipant. The participant is the sole lifecycle owner; Utilities renders the package contribution factory with the injected controller context instead of duplicating metadata or dispatch. The app supplies injected global NSEvent hotkey, SnapCaptureKit/Workbench capture, lazy AVFoundation camera-preview, and Desktop Curtain adapters. The right ⌘ + right ⌥ chord offers screenshot, video, or GIF-source output for a display, window, or area, with an optional backdrop. GIF export stays parked until the shared Studio engine gains an encoder. PENDING: real-permission chord and capture verification on device.

caveats.note: Not surfaced in Indoor — the Utilities hub stays hidden in App Store builds.
Alpha-only: global capture chord (right ⌘ + right ⌥) on the Outdoor macOS alpha lane; video/GIF-source recordings drive the shared Studio (Workbench) engine.

goldStandard.gap: FeatureQuickCapture owns palette/runtime orchestration, chord policy, UI contribution, and lifecycle; app shell retains injected hotkey, SnapCaptureKit/Workbench capture, AVFoundation camera, and Desktop Curtain adapters

features.name: Skip HealthKit workout logging
features.description: Code-only and default OFF via FeatureFlags.enableSkipHealthKitLogging. Manual follow-up required before enabling: HealthKit App ID capability/provisioning for Indoor and Outdoor, com.apple.developer.healthkit entitlement, and NSHealthUpdateUsageDescription.

goldStandard.gap: logging logic lives behind a package seam; app side is a thin seam but wired directly, and entitlement/provisioning follow-up still gates shipping — no registry seam

features.name: Content Safety Filter (toggle)
features.description: FeatureContentSafety owns filter prompt composition, category verdicts, full-text checks, streaming scanner state, effective enablement policy, and hosted chat enforcement calls. The app shell only supplies persisted setting, subscription, and age-verification inputs plus Settings UI. Disabling is Pro + age-gated (Apple DeclaredAgeRange API, OS 26+).

goldStandard.gap: all filter logic + enforcement packaged; app supplies only persisted setting/subscription/age inputs + Settings UI, but wired directly rather than via a provider/registry seam

features.name: Subscription / Payments
features.description: Indoor = StoreKit 2; Outdoor = Polar. Beta = Pro unlocked free (data-for-Pro exchange). FeatureSubscription owns BillingProvider, external-license, activation, KVS, subscription UI, and the sole Settings UIContribution registered under id=account. FeatureSubscriptionStoreKit owns StoreKit transaction and promoted-purchase observation, product loading, purchase, restore, verification, refund filtering, and environment trust. FeatureSubscriptionPolar owns Polar validation/token networking. The app remains a thin coordinator that injects UserDefaults/iCloud KVS storage, build configuration and legal links, plus the existing effective-access seam for debug and prerelease Pro grants. Feature catalog requirements.minTier remains the source of feature-tier gating; grace-period and entitlement resolution semantics are unchanged.

caveats.note: Payments via StoreKit 2.
Payments via Polar; external-license activation synced via iCloud.
Pro unlocked free while Sentry diagnostics stay ON (isBetaProPerkActive, #if BETA) — a data-for-Pro exchange; turning off beta-Sentry drops the perk.

features.package: FeatureSubscription + flavor-specific billing providers + app adapters

goldStandard.gap: billing lifecycle and account UI are package-owned by build flavor; the app supplies only persistence, feature-flag, and effective-access adapters

api.reason: Purchase and restore flows are policy-excluded from HTTP control.

features.name: Beta Telemetry (Sentry) — GOLD STANDARD MODULE
features.description: Reference module for the modular architecture. Facade always present; the Sentry impl is linked/injected by the macOS Beta target — which BOTH the beta channel AND the alpha overlay (Beta scheme + ALPHA) build on, so telemetry runs on alpha AND beta (not beta-only). Alpha is mandatory (opt-out ignored); beta is opt-out via betaDisableSentry. Stable Outdoor + App Store link no Sentry backend at all. Release flows bake SENTRY_DSN (into Info.plist); unseeded local builds no-op. Events are tagged with environment=alpha/beta + release=<version>+<build>.

caveats.note: Facade present, no Sentry backend linked — no telemetry.
Facade present, no Sentry backend linked — no telemetry.

ALWAYS ON: the Sentry impl (linked via the Beta target the alpha overlay builds on) is mandatory in alpha — the opt-out is ignored, and that unconditional diagnostics consent is what makes the free-Pro perk unconditional too.

On by default, OPT-OUT via betaDisableSentry (that consent is the free-Pro trigger). Same Sentry impl as alpha, injected by the macOS Beta target; SENTRY_DSN baked by the release flow (unseeded local builds no-op).

api.reason: Telemetry consent and diagnostics are deliberately not remote-controllable.

features.name: Translate
features.description: Standalone on-device translation with FluidAudio diarization, contextual translation, and interlinear gloss. Optional output modules consume TranslateDisplayContent through TranslateDisplayAdapter; the feature remains fully usable when no adapter is registered.

goldStandard.gap: FeatureTranslate owns its UI, view model, conversation/diarization services, and feature-neutral TranslateDisplayAdapter registry; it has no dependency on the Even Realities driver

features.name: Even Realities / G2 display driver
features.description: FeatureEvenRealities is a feature-agnostic display driver compiled and runtime-registered in Indoor, Outdoor, and Beta, matching the historical FEATURE_EVEN_G2 composition. EvenRealitiesTranslate is the first optional adapter and the only place TranslateDisplayContent maps to GlassesDisplayContent. Removing that adapter leaves both Translate and the driver independently usable. Physical G2 BLE verification remains pending.

goldStandard.gap: FeatureEvenRealities owns the generic GlassesDisplaySink, G2 driver, listener, Live Activity, companion surface, and LaunchParticipant lifecycle; feature adapters are separate products

features.name: LM Studio model interop
features.description: macOS / Outdoor + Beta only. LMStudioModelSource (walk ~/.lmstudio/models/<org>/<model>, validate against the app completeness rule, reject partial/.part/sharded-without-index, route GGUF-only dirs to the GGUF backend via format=.gguf, infer capabilities from config.json) is packaged in LocalAICore and unit-tested with fixtures + the real on-disk library. The LocalAIExternalModelServing provider protocol and registry + GET /v1/local/models/external, POST /external/import (idempotent symlink + .userAdded catalog contribution), POST /refresh, and the GET /v1/models merge live in LocalAIHTTPServer (route-tested). The app registers LocalAIExternalModelService at composition time; it owns only the symlink link-in (~/Library/Caches/models/<org>/<model> → the LM Studio dir; never clobbers a real dir), .userAdded projection, and HubSnapshotDownloader's local-first short-circuit so a complete local/imported model loads offline without a HubApi network revision-check. Deferred: surfacing imported models in the model-manager/picker UI with a provenance badge.

caveats.note: Not shipped — the sandboxed App Store build can't read ~/.lmstudio or symlink into the model cache; #if !APPSTORE_BUILD && os(macOS) strips the source + routes (they 503).

Discovers ~/.lmstudio/models MLX models, imports a chosen one via symlink into the model cache (no 15-32 GB re-download), and serves it through /v1/local/models/external, /external/import, /refresh, and the /v1/models merge.

goldStandard.gap: terminal symlink and HubApi adapters remain app-owned; LocalAIExternalModelServing is registered through the packaged provider registry

features.name: GGUF backend (llama.cpp sidecar)
features.description: macOS / Outdoor + Beta only. Sidecar-first (see docs/planning/done/gguf-backend-spike-notes.md): FeatureGGUF's LlamaServerSidecar actor locates a llama-server binary, spawns it on a free loopback port for the selected .gguf model, health-checks /health, detects crashes and restarts (bounded), and terminates gracefully. GGUFChatBackend proxies /v1/chat/completions SSE into LocalAITextGenerationEvents behind the SAME LocalAITextGenerating seam RemoteCatBackend uses, registered through ChatBackendRegistry with a `gguf:`-prefixed model contributor. GGUF-only LM Studio dirs are no longer rejected by ExternalModelSource — they carry format=.gguf and are served here instead of the MLX loader. Physically absent from App Store + iOS (package unlinked, FEATURE_GGUF undefined). Deferred: bundling llama-server (size + signing/notarization) and GGUF model-download UX.

caveats.note: Not shipped — the sandboxed App Store build can't spawn a child process; FeatureGGUF is unlinked (FEATURE_GGUF undefined) so it is physically absent from the binary.
Discovers .gguf files in the model cache + LM Studio library and serves a selected one via a locally-spawned llama-server (OpenAI-compatible loopback).

goldStandard.gap: sidecar lifecycle + SSE adaptation + gguf discovery + ChatBackendRegistry registration are packaged; the app supplies only binary/model-dir config at the composition root behind FEATURE_GGUF

features.name: omlx sidecar (Python MLX oracle)
features.description: macOS / Alpha only. Bridge track from docs/planning/done/omlx-sidecar-plan.md: FeatureOMLXSidecar's SidecarProcess spawns/health-polls/tears down the omlx server, SidecarBackend adapts its /v1/chat/completions SSE (incl. reasoning_content + streamed tool_calls) into LocalAITextGenerationEvents, and SidecarRouting sends big/coding downloaded MLX models to the sidecar (H1: unloads the in-process runtime first). OMLXSidecarRegistration owns ChatBackendRegistry registration, while OMLXSidecarTerminationParticipant owns process teardown; no app-side sidecar startup or parallel lifecycle path remains. Engine policy via localAI.engineSelectionMode (auto/forceNative/forceSidecar) in Advanced Settings lets us A/B the native runtime vs omlx through the same app surfaces. Deferred: S4 runtime bundling (currently needs a dev-machine omlx venv).

caveats.note: Not shipped — sandboxed builds can't spawn a child process; FEATURE_OMLX_SIDECAR undefined so the code is physically absent.

Dev/compare surface: routes big/coding MLX models through a managed omlx subprocess behind the LocalAITextGenerating seam. Default OFF (localAI.engineSelectionMode=auto); requires a local omlx install until S4 bundling.

goldStandard.gap: FeatureOMLXSidecar owns process lifecycle, SSE adaptation, routing policy, ChatBackendRegistry registration, and termination; the app injects only native-runtime unload and logging hooks

features.name: Device Snap Capture (timestamped multi-source screenshots + transcript)
features.description: macOS / Alpha only. docs/planning/GOAL-device-snap-capture.md. SnapCaptureKit's pure target owns the multi-source SnapSessionWriter/README/clipboard-path, SnapSessionController, SnapCaptureSource seam, and additive transcription event contract. Its macOS SnapCapturePlatform target owns SnapCaptureCoordinator, SnapWindowCaptureSource (SCScreenshotManager), SnapDeviceCaptureSource, the device preview manager/windows, and the DEBUG -snap_device_spike runner over SnapDeviceCaptureKit's CoreMediaIO/AVFoundation DAL implementation. SnapCaptureLaunchParticipant is the sole owner of device enumeration and transcription-observer startup/teardown; the old QuickCaptureFeatureController direct lifecycle call is gone and singleton resolution stays lazy. The existing `.quickCapture` Utilities UIContribution retains the same order/action but is now catalogued to snap-capture while requiring the shared Quick Capture host. Snapshot behavior and paths are unchanged. PENDING: live DAL verification that a trusted/unlocked cabled iPhone supplies its screen rather than camera, plus S3 overlay ergonomics. Camera prompt occurs on device-window open; Screen Recording permission occurs on window/display capture.

caveats.note: Not shipped — capture/AX gated #if os(macOS) && !APPSTORE_BUILD; physically inert in the sandboxed build.

WIP: ⌘⌥ overlay opens iPhone-SCREEN / window / display previews; Screenshot during a transcription writes timestamped PNGs correlated to the audio clock; on stop a session folder path lands on the clipboard. Camera (iPhone DAL) + Screen Recording (windows/displays).

goldStandard.gap: SnapCaptureKit owns the session model, transcription event contract, capture-source seam, concrete ScreenCaptureKit/AVFoundation adapters, coordinator, preview UI, and lazy lifecycle participant; the app retains only composition and the existing Quick Capture host

features.name: Studio recording & streaming
features.description: macOS / Alpha only. The /v1/local/studio/* recording & streaming suite: StudioModeKit owns word-timed recording bundles and session lifecycle, StudioSceneKit the scene compositor, SubtitleKit + TranscriptSyncKit the subtitle rendering and transcript-to-audio-clock sync, DevLoopUI + DevLoopStreamKit the dev-loop streaming surface, and StudioAgentBridge the headless agent control bridge. It consumes the shared capture engine (FrameSourceKit/FramePipelineKit) and VLMDispatchKit rather than owning frame sources. Drives real displays/windows/devices headlessly over the token-gated Local API studio routes; no human-at-the-machine capture required. PENDING: on-device verification of the full record→composite→stream pipeline under real TCC grants.

caveats.note: Not shipped — the recording/streaming suite is alpha-lane only and absent from App Store builds.
Not shipped by the standard Outdoor target — alpha overlay only.
Alpha overlay only. The /v1/local/studio/* routes drive real capture headlessly (word-timed recording bundles, scene compositing, subtitles, transcript sync, dev-loop streaming, agent bridge).

features.package: StudioModeKit + StudioSceneKit + StudioAgentBridge + DevLoop* + Subtitle/TranscriptSync + app composition

goldStandard.gap: StudioModeKit/StudioSceneKit/StudioAgentBridge/DevLoop*/Subtitle/TranscriptSync own the recording, compositing, subtitle, and streaming engines; the app supplies only composition-root wiring and the shared capture engine (FrameSourceKit/FramePipelineKit) it consumes

modules.description: Migration-gate and scenario test scaffolding for package/refactor validation.
